revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''双方中円墳'''（そうほうちゅうえんふん）とは、[[古墳]]の形のひとつ。 == 特徴 == 円丘の両側に方形の突出部を持ち...'
revision.text:
'''双方中円墳'''（そうほうちゅうえんふん）とは、[[古墳]]の形のひとつ。
== 特徴 == 円丘の両側に方形の突出部を持ち、比較的[[古墳時代]]前期に見られる。 {{substub}}

'''双方中円墳'''（そうほうちゅうえんふん）とは、[[古墳]]の形のひとつ。
== 特徴 == 円丘の両側に方形の突出部を持ち、比較的[[古墳時代]]前期に見られる。
== 代表的な古墳 == *[[楯築遺跡]] *[[櫛山古墳]]
{{substub}}

[[画像:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|双方中円墳の例]] '''双方中円墳'''（そうほうちゅうえんふん）とは、[[古墳]]の形のひとつ。
== 特徴 == 円丘の両側に方形の突出部を持ち、比較的[[古墳時代]]前期に見られる。
== 代表的な古墳 == *[[楯築遺跡]] *[[櫛山古墳]]
{{substub}}

[[画像:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|双方中円墳の例（楯築遺跡）]] '''双方中円墳'''（そうほうちゅうえんふん）とは、[[古墳]]の形のひとつ。
== 特徴 == 円丘の両側に方形の突出部を持ち、比較的[[古墳時代]]前期に見られる。
== 代表的な古墳 == *[[楯築遺跡]] *[[櫛山古墳]]
{{substub}}

revision.comment: カテゴリ付加、猫塚古墳追加
revision.text:
[[画像:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|双方中円墳の例（櫛山古墳）]] '''双方中円墳'''（そうほうちゅうえんふん）とは、[[古墳]]の形のひとつ。
== 特徴 == 円丘の両側に方形の突出部を持ち、比較的[[古墳時代]]前期に見られる。
== 代表的な古墳 == *[[楯築遺跡]] *[[櫛山古墳]] *猫塚古墳（[[石清尾山古墳群]]） - 香川県高松市
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[古墳]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:そうほうちゆうえんふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:考古学]] [[Category:日本の古墳]] [[Category:古墳時代]]

[[画像:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|双方中円墳の例（櫛山古墳）]] '''双方中円墳'''（そうほうちゅうえんふん）とは、[[古墳]]の形のひとつ。
== 特徴 == 円丘の両側に方形の突出部を持ち、比較的[[古墳時代]]前期に見られる。
== 代表的な古墳 == *[[楯築遺跡]] *[[櫛山古墳]] *猫塚古墳（[[石清尾山古墳群]]） - [[香川県]][[高松市]]
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[古墳]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:そうほうちゆうえんふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:考古学]] [[Category:日本の古墳]] [[Category:古墳時代]]

[[画像:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|双方中円墳の例（櫛山古墳）]] '''双方中円墳'''（そうほうちゅうえんふん）とは、[[古墳]]の形のひとつ。
== 特徴 == 円丘の両側に方形の突出部を持ち、比較的[[古墳時代]]前期に見られる。 または、一般的な前方後円墳の後円部後方に、さらに短い方形部を軸線を一致させて付設したものである。厳密にはどちらが正面になるのか明らかでない<ref>泉森皎「櫛山古墳」 文化庁文化財保護部史跡研究会監修『図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3』同朋舎出版 1991年 55ページ</ref>。
== 代表的な古墳 == *[[楯築遺跡]] *[[櫛山古墳]] *猫塚古墳（[[石清尾山古墳群]]） - [[香川県]][[高松市]]
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[古墳]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:そうほうちゆうえんふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:考古学]] [[Category:日本の古墳]] [[Category:古墳時代]]

[[画像:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|双方中円墳の例（櫛山古墳）]] '''双方中円墳'''（そうほうちゅうえんふん）とは、[[古墳]]の形のひとつ。
== 特徴 == 円丘の両側に方形の突出部を持ち、比較的[[古墳時代]]前期に見られる。 または、一般的な前方後円墳の後円部後方に、さらに短い方形部を軸線を一致させて付設したものである。厳密にはどちらが正面になるのか明らかでない<ref>泉森皎「櫛山古墳」 文化庁文化財保護部史跡研究会監修『図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3』同朋舎出版 1991年 55ページ</ref>。
== 代表的な古墳 == *[[楯築遺跡]] *[[櫛山古墳]] *猫塚古墳（[[石清尾山古墳群]]） - [[香川県]][[高松市]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[古墳]] *[[円墳]] *[[方墳]] *[[前方後方墳]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:そうほうちゆうえんふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:考古学]] [[Category:日本の古墳]] [[Category:古墳時代]]

[[画像:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|双方中円墳の例（櫛山古墳）]] '''双方中円墳'''（そうほうちゅうえんふん）とは、[[古墳]]の形のひとつ。
== 特徴 == 円丘の両側に方形の突出部を持ち、比較的[[古墳時代]]前期に見られる。 または、一般的な前方後円墳の後円部後方に、さらに短い方形部を軸線を一致させて付設したものである。厳密にはどちらが正面になるのか明らかでない<ref>泉森皎「櫛山古墳」 文化庁文化財保護部史跡研究会監修『図説 日本の史跡 第3巻 原始3』同朋舎出版 1991年 55ページ</ref>。
== 代表的な古墳 == *[[楯築遺跡]] *[[櫛山古墳]] *猫塚古墳（[[石清尾山古墳群]]） -（[[香川県]][[高松市]]、鏡塚古墳など3世紀後半から4世紀前半に若干の例がある。）
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[古墳]] *[[円墳]] *[[方墳]] *[[前方後方墳]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:そうほうちゆうえんふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:考古学]] [[Category:日本の古墳]] [[Category:古墳時代]]

[[画像:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|双方中円墳の例（櫛山古墳）
{{国土航空写真}}]] '''双方中円墳'''（そうほうちゅうえんふん）とは、[[古墳]]の形のひとつ。 == 特徴 == 円丘の両側に方形の突出部を持ち、比較的[[古墳時代]]前期に見られる。 または、一般的な前方後円墳の後円部後方に、さらに短い方形部を軸線を一致させて付設したものである。厳密にはどちらが正面になるのか明らかでない泉森皎「櫛山古墳」　文化庁文化財保護部史跡研究会監修『図説　日本の史跡　第3巻　原始3』同朋舎出版　1991年　55ページ。 == 代表的な古墳 == *[[楯築遺跡]] *[[櫛山古墳]] *猫塚古墳（[[石清尾山古墳群]]） -（[[香川県]][[高松市]]、鏡塚古墳など3世紀後半から4世紀前半に若干の例がある。） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[古墳]] *[[円墳]] *[[方墳]] *[[前方後方墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:そうほうちゆうえんふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:考古学]] [[Category:日本の古墳]] [[Category:古墳時代]]

[[画像:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|双方中円墳の例（櫛山古墳）
{{国土航空写真}}]] '''双方中円墳'''（そうほうちゅうえんふん）とは、[[古墳]]の形のひとつ。 == 特徴 == 円丘の両側に方形の突出部を持ち、比較的[[古墳時代]]前期に見られる。 または、一般的な前方後円墳の後円部後方に、さらに短い方形部を軸線を一致させて付設したものである。厳密にはどちらが正面になるのか明らかでない泉森皎「櫛山古墳」　文化庁文化財保護部史跡研究会監修『図説　日本の史跡　第3巻　原始3』同朋舎出版　1991年　55ページ。 == 代表的な古墳 == *[[楯築遺跡]] *[[櫛山古墳]] *猫塚古墳（[[石清尾山古墳群]]） -（[[香川県]][[高松市]]、鏡塚古墳など3世紀後半から4世紀前半に若干の例がある。） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[古墳]] *[[円墳]] *[[方墳]] *[[前方後方墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:そうほうちゆうえんふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:日本の考古学]] [[Category:日本の古墳]] [[Category:古墳時代]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}})}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Ishizuchi Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain burial mounds also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): * '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} ### Related Site: * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Burial Mound}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Soho-chuen-fun-qq]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[Category:Kofun Template Forks]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Soho-chuen-fun-qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]]

{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Ishizuchi Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain burial mounds also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): * '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} ### Related Site: * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Burial Mound}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Soho-chuen-fun-qq]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[Category:Kofun Template Forks]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Soho-chuen-fun-qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]]

Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} ### Related Site: * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Burial Mound}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Soho-chuen-fun-qq]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[Category:Kofun Template Forks]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Soho-chuen-fun-qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]]

A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Soho-chuen-fun-qq]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[Category:Kofun Template Forks]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Soho-chuen-fun-qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]]

[[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}})}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Ishizuchi Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain burial mounds also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): * '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} ### Related Site: * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Burial Mound}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Soho-chuen-fun-qq]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[Category:Kofun Template Forks]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Soho-chuen-fun-qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]]

[[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}})}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Ishizuchi Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain burial mounds also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): * '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} ### Related Site: * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Burial Mound}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Soho-chuen-fun-qq]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[Category:Kofun Template Forks]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Soho-chuen-fun-qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]]

While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain burial mounds also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): * '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} ### Related Site: * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Burial Mound}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Soho-chuen-fun-qq]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[Category:Kofun Template Forks]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Soho-chuen-fun-qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Soho-chuen-fun]][[Category:11]]

[[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}})}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Ishizuchi Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain burial mounds also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): * '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} ### Related Site: * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Burial Mound}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain burial mounds also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): * '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} ### Related Site: * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Burial Mound}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] revision.text:
[[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}})}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Ishizuchi Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain burial mounds also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): * '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} ### Related Site: * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Burial Mound}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] revision.text:
[[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}})}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Ishizuchi Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain burial mounds also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): * '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} ### Related Site: * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Burial Mound}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}})}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Ishizuchi Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain burial mounds also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): * '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Burial Mound}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}})}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Ishizuchi Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain burial mounds also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): * '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Burial Mound}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain burial mounds also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): * '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Burial Mound}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}})}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Ishizuchi Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mound. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain burial mounds also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): * '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Burial Mound}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}})}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Ishizuchi Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mound. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain burial mounds also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): * '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Burial Mound}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Burial Mound}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] revision.text:
[[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}})}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Ishizuchi Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mound. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain burial mounds also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): * '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Burial Mound}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}})}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Ishizuchi Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mound. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain burial mounds also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): * '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Burial Mound}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]]
