[[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}})}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Ishizuchi Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mound. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain burial mounds also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): * '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Burial Mound}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}})}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Ishizuchi Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mound. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain burial mounds also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): * '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Burial Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Burial Mound}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[Category:Kofun Template Forks]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:oho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:hashtag replacing]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain burial mounds also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): * '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) - '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[Category:Kofun Template Forks]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:oho-chuen-fun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:hashtag replacing]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain Kofuns also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): * '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. * '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[Category:Kofun Template Forks]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:oho-chuen-fun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:hashtag replacing]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:11]]

{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Ishizuchi Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain Kofuns also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): ** '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}})}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Ishizuchi Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain Kofuns also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): ** '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) - A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen [[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}})}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Ishizuchi Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain Kofuns also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): ** '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) ** A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}''', certain Kofuns also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}): ** '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) ** A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] revision.text:
[[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}})}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=draft}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=draft}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=draft}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳|12=draft}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Ishizuchi Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|12=draft}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=draft}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} Kofun. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳|12=draft}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}}''', certain Kofuns also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}): ** '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳|12=draft}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳|12=draft}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳|12=draft}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) ** A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun Template Forks]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]] [[sn:oho-chuen-fun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}}

[[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}})}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=draft}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=draft}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=draft}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳|12=draft}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Ishizuchi Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|12=draft}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=draft}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} Kofun. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳|12=draft}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}}''', certain Kofuns also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}): ** '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳|12=draft}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳|12=draft}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳|12=draft}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) ** A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun Template Forks]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]] [[sn:oho-chuen-fun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}}

It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳|12=draft}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}}''', certain Kofuns also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}): ** '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳|12=draft}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳|12=draft}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳|12=draft}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki, Okayama|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) ** A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun Template Forks]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[sn:Sōhō-chūen Kofun]] [[sn:oho-chuen-fun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōhō-chūen Kofun}}

These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{座標一覧}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|ja|明合古墳|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|ja|津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|石清尾山古墳群|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu, Kagawa|ja|高松市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}): ** '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳|12=draft}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳|12=draft}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳|12=draft}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. [[ja:双方中円墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11410445]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] ([[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]])}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491397}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label=[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=Sōhō-chūen Kofun.svg|marksize=18|label=[[:en:Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster]]|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''[[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]''', certain Kofuns also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{GeoGroup}} * '''[[:en:Akeai Kofun|Akeai Kofun]]''' ([[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * '''[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]''' ([[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the [[:en:Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster]]''' ([[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu, Kagawa]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]): ** '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekozuka Kofun|qq=}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamizuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}''' ([[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) ** A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|双方中円墳|version=99286115|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11410445}}

It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''[[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]''', certain Kofuns also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{GeoGroup}} * '''[[:en:Akeai Kofun|Akeai Kofun]]''' ([[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * '''[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]''' ([[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the [[:en:Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster]]''' ([[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu, Kagawa]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]): ** '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekozuka Kofun|qq=}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamizuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}''' ([[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) ** A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|双方中円墳|version=99286115|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11410445}}

[[ja:双方中円墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11410445]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] ([[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]])}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=双方中円墳.svg|marksize=18|label=[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=双方中円墳.svg|marksize=18|label=[[:en:Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster]]|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''[[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]''', certain Kofuns also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{GeoGroup}} * '''[[:en:Akeai Kofun|Akeai Kofun]]''' ([[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * '''[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]''' ([[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the [[:en:Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster]]''' ([[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu, Kagawa]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]): ** '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekozuka Kofun|qq=}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamizuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}''' ([[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) ** A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|双方中円墳|version=99286115|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11410445}}

[[ja:双方中円墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11410445]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] ([[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]])}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=双方中円墳.svg|marksize=18|label=[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=双方中円墳.svg|marksize=18|label=[[:en:Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster]]|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''[[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]''', certain Kofuns also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{GeoGroup}} * '''[[:en:Akeai Kofun|Akeai Kofun]]''' ([[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * '''[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]''' ([[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the [[:en:Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster]]''' ([[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu, Kagawa]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]): ** '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekozuka Kofun|qq=}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamizuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}''' ([[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) ** A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|双方中円墳|version=99286115|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11410445}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:双方中円墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11410445]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] ([[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]])}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=双方中円墳.svg|marksize=18|label=[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=双方中円墳.svg|marksize=18|label=[[:en:Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster]]|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''[[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]''', certain Kofuns also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{GeoGroup}} * '''[[:en:Akeai Kofun|Akeai Kofun]]''' ([[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * '''[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]''' ([[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the [[:en:Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster]]''' ([[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu, Kagawa]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]): ** '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekozuka Kofun|qq=}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamizuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}''' ([[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) ** A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|双方中円墳|version=99286115|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11410445}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
