While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''[[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]''', certain Kofuns also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{GeoGroup}} * '''[[:en:Akeai Kofun|Akeai Kofun]]''' ([[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * '''[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]''' ([[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the [[:en:Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster]]''' ([[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu, Kagawa]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]): ** '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekozuka Kofun|qq=}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamizuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}''' ([[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) ** A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|双方中円墳|version=99286115|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11410445}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:双方中円墳]] [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[ja:双方中円墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11410445]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] ([[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]])}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=双方中円墳.svg|marksize=18|label=[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=双方中円墳.svg|marksize=18|label=[[:en:Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster]]|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''[[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]''', certain Kofuns also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{GeoGroup}} * '''[[:en:Akeai Kofun|Akeai Kofun]]''' ([[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * '''[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]''' ([[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the [[:en:Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster]]''' ([[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu, Kagawa]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]): ** '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekozuka Kofun|qq=}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamizuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}''' ([[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) ** A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|双方中円墳|version=99286115|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11410445}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''[[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]''', certain Kofuns also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{GeoGroup}} * '''[[:en:Akeai Kofun|Akeai Kofun]]''' ([[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * '''[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]''' ([[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the [[:en:Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster]]''' ([[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu, Kagawa]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]): ** '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekozuka Kofun|qq=}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamizuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}''' ([[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) ** A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|双方中円墳|version=99286115|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:双方中円墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11410445}} [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{GeoGroup}} * '''[[:en:Akeai Kofun|Akeai Kofun]]''' ([[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * '''[[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]''' ([[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the [[:en:Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster]]''' ([[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu, Kagawa]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]): ** '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekozuka Kofun|qq=}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamizuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}''' ([[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) ** A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|双方中円墳|version=99286115|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:双方中円墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11410445}} [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki, Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) ** A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|双方中円墳|version=99286115|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:双方中円墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11410445}} [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:双方中円墳]] [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[ja:双方中円墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11410445]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun}} ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}})}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=双方中円墳.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=双方中円墳.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|en|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11586812|lt=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} Kofun. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}}''', certain Kofuns also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{GeoGroup}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|en|Akeai Kofun|WD=Q11512013|lt=Akeai Kofun}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|en|Tsu, Mie|WD=Q203027|lt=Tsu, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|en|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11586812|lt=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}): ** '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekozuka Kofun|qq=}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamizuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki, Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) ** A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|双方中円墳|version=99286115|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:双方中円墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11410445}} [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}}''', certain Kofuns also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{GeoGroup}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|en|Akeai Kofun|WD=Q11512013|lt=Akeai Kofun}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|en|Tsu, Mie|WD=Q203027|lt=Tsu, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|en|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11586812|lt=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}): ** '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekozuka Kofun|qq=}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamizuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki, Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) ** A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|双方中円墳|version=99286115|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:双方中円墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11410445}} [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki, Okayama|1=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) ** A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|双方中円墳|version=99286115|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:双方中円墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11410445}} [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:双方中円墳]] [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[ja:双方中円墳]] [[File:Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun|1=Kushiyama Kofun}} ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri, Nara|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}})}}{{small|One of the {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397|1=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan}}.}}]] {{Location map+|Japan|width=250|float=right|caption={{Center|Distribution of {{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}}}|places= {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=39|lat_sec=47.01|lon_deg=133|lon_min=49|lon_sec=31.76|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=7|label={{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}|label_size=80|position=top}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=24.68|lon_deg=135|lon_min=51|lon_sec=7.54|mark=双方中円墳.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun|1=Kushiyama Kofun}}|label_size=80|position=right}} {{Location map~|Japan|lat_deg=34|lat_min=19|lat_sec=39.63|lon_deg=134|lon_min=1|lon_sec=25.08|mark=双方中円墳.svg|marksize=18|label={{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|en|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11586812|lt=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}|label_size=80|position=bottom}} }} The '''{{ill|Sōhō-chūen Kofun|ja|双方中円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōhō-chūen Kofun|qq=}}''' (そうほうちゅうえんふん) is a type of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} Kofun. It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun|1=Kushiyama Kofun}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}''', certain Kofuns also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{GeoGroup}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|en|Akeai Kofun|WD=Q11512013|lt=Akeai Kofun|1=Akeai Kofun}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|en|Tsu, Mie|WD=Q203027|lt=Tsu, Mie|1=Tsu, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun|1=Kushiyama Kofun}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri, Nara|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|en|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11586812|lt=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu, Kagawa|1=Takamatsu}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}): ** '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekozuka Kofun|qq=}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamizuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki, Okayama|1=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) ** A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|双方中円墳|version=99286115|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:双方中円墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11410445}} [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

It is characterized by a circular main mound with '''two rectangular extensions''', one on each side. == Overview == Whereas '''{{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}}''' (keyhole-shaped kofun) feature a '''single''' rectangular extension at the front of a circular main mound, the '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun''' has '''two''' such extensions, one on either side.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} These kofun are seen primarily in the early '''{{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}'''. While '''Zenpō-kōen-fun''' typically have a designated '''frontal direction''', it is unclear which side was considered the "front" in '''Sōhō-chūen Kofun'''.{{Sfn|Izumimori|1991|p=55}} As of 2015, only '''four confirmed examples''' of Sōhō-chūen Kofun are known in Japan.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} Among these, '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun|1=Kushiyama Kofun}}''' is notable for having '''one smaller extension''' compared to the other.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} Furthermore, during the '''{{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}''', certain Kofuns also exhibited '''keyhole-shaped''' or '''Sōhō-chūen-like''' structures. These early forms likely '''evolved into the standardized keyhole-shaped and Sōhō-chūen kofun''' of the Kofun period.{{Sfn|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}} == List of Known Sōhō-chūen Kofun == {{GeoGroup}} * '''{{ill|Akeai Kofun|en|Akeai Kofun|WD=Q11512013|lt=Akeai Kofun|1=Akeai Kofun}}''' ({{ill|Tsu, Mie|en|Tsu, Mie|WD=Q203027|lt=Tsu, Mie|1=Tsu, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun|1=Kushiyama Kofun}}''' ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri, Nara|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|33|24.68|N|135|51|7.54|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Kushiyama Kofun}}) * '''Three tombs in the {{ill|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|en|Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11586812|lt=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster|1=Iwaseoyama Kofun Cluster}}''' ({{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu, Kagawa|1=Takamatsu}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}): ** '''{{ill|Nekozuka Kofun|ja|猫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekozuka Kofun|qq=}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|39.63|N|134|1|25.08|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Nekozuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Kagamizuka Kofun|ja|鏡塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamizuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|55.72|N|134|1|47.73|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kagamizuka Kofun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. ** '''{{ill|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja|稲荷山北端1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ({{Coord|34|19|58.07|N|134|2|22.32|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}}) * '''Pile-stone tomb (積石塚)'''. Previously believed to be a '''Zenpō-kōen-fun (前方後円墳)''', but '''confirmed as a Sōhō-chūen Kofun in 2015'''.{{Sfn|Inariyama Hokutan 1-gō Fun}} === Related Site: === * '''{{ill|Tatezuka Kofun|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatezuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}}''' ({{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki, Okayama|1=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ウィキ座標|34|39|47.01|N|133|49|31.76|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|name=Tatezuka Kofun}}) ** A '''Yayoi-period''' '''Sōhō-chūen-style''' '''mounded tomb (墳丘墓)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8F%8C%E6%96%B9%E4%B8%AD%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3-89694 "Sōhō-chūen Kofun"] in *Sekai Daikyakka Jiten (World Encyclopedia)*, Heibonsha (Link via Asahi Shimbun's *Kotobank*)|ref={{Harvid|Sōhō-chūen Kofun (Sekai Daikyakka)}}}}. {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|双方中円墳|version=99286115|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:双方中円墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11410445}} [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
